articles.title: Report: Many young Moroccans enter teaching out of necessity, not passion
articles.summary: Tens of thousands of young Moroccans are choosing teaching as a profession not out of passion or a sense of educational mission, but as a last resort to escape unemployment and limited career prospects, according to a new report by Morocco’s Supreme Council for Education, Training, and Scientific Research (CSEFRS). articles.title: Qatar says diplomatic efforts between Iran and the need 'more time'
articles.summary: Qatar says diplomatic efforts between Iran and the need 'more time' Qatar stated on Tuesday that negotiations between the United States and Iran needed "more time" to succeed, after US President Donald Trump announced he had called off another attack against the Islamic Republic. "We support the diplomatic efforts led by Pakistan...to bring the parties closer together and find a solution, and we believe they require more time," said Qatari foreign ministry spokesman Majed al-Ansari at a press conference in Doha.

Irish president 'very worried' about sister detained by Israel
articles.summary: Irish president 'very worried' about sister detained by Israel Irish President Catherine Connolly said she was "very worried" about her sister, who was detained by Israeli forces on Monday while aboard a flotilla bound for Gaza. Margaret Connolly is one of at least six Irish citizens aboard a vessel carrying aid that was seized alongside 10 other boats off the coast of Cyprus.  “It’s quite upsetting, and I’m very worried about her, and I’m also very concerned about her colleagues on board," the president told reporters. She added that she was "proud" of her sister.
